welcome.title: Welcome to Hagicode Desktop
welcome.description: Hagicode Desktop is the local launcher for Hagicode, allowing you to quickly use Hagicode locally. We'll guide you through a five-step setup in about 3-5 minutes.

manage.title: Version Management
manage.description: Manage Hagicode Server installation and updates

monitor.title: Service Monitoring
monitor.description: Start, stop, and monitor service status

steps.welcome: Review the setup and what will happen next
steps.download: Download the latest Hagicode Server package
steps.launch: Start the service and confirm it is running

complete.title: Download Complete!
complete.message: Package file verified. You can now continue to launch the service.

dependencies.title: Install System Dependencies
dependencies.subtitle: Hagicode requires certain system dependencies to function properly.
dependencies.description: Check the installation status of system dependencies

complete.title: Dependencies Installed
complete.message: All dependencies have been successfully installed.

error.message: Some dependencies failed to install. Please install them manually and continue.

depInstallConfirm.title: Install Missing Dependencies
depInstallConfirm.versionMismatchNote: Some dependencies have incorrect versions and need to be upgraded.
depInstallConfirm.mixedMissingMessage: Detected {{missing}} missing and {{mismatch}} version mismatched dependency
depInstallConfirm.mixedMissingMessage_other: Detected {{missing}} missing and {{mismatch}} version mismatched dependencies
depInstallConfirm.upgradeNote: Version mismatched dependencies will be upgraded to the required version.
depInstallConfirm.permissionWarning: Installing these dependencies may require administrator privileges.

complete.title: Setup Complete!
complete.subtitle: Hagicode Desktop has been successfully installed and started.
complete.success: Congratulations! Hagicode Desktop has been successfully installed and started.
complete.ready: You can now start using all features.

whatsNext.title: You can now:
whatsNext.manage: Manage Hagicode Server from the main interface

failure.title: Startup Failed
failure.subtitle: Hagicode Server could not finish starting. Review the diagnostics and reinstall before continuing setup.
failure.guidance: Open the full log, then reinstall the current version and restart the setup flow.
failure.emptySummary: No startup failure summary was captured
failure.emptyLog: No startup failure log was captured
failure.truncatedHint: The startup log was truncated to the most recent output.
failure.recoveryHint: We recommend reinstalling the current version and resetting onboarding before trying setup again.
failure.copyError: Failed to copy startup failure log
failure.copyEmpty: There is no startup failure log to copy

skip.title: Confirm Skip Setup
skip.description: Are you sure you want to skip the first-time setup?
skip.manualInstallNote:
After skipping, you can:
• Manually install Hagicode Server through "Package Management" in the main interface
• Re-enable the setup process in settings
skip.reenableNote: If this is your first time using Hagicode Desktop, we recommend completing the setup process.

dependencyCheck.title: Dependency Check Results
dependencyCheck.summary: Checked {{total}} {{total, plural, =1 {dependency} other {dependencies}}}, {{passed}} {{passed, plural, =1 {passed} other {passed}}}, {{failed}} {{failed, plural, =1 {needs attention} other {need attention}}}

openspec.title: Install OpenSpec
openspec.description: OpenSpec is required before you continue to download and launch Hagicode Server. Use the one-click install first, then verify the installed version on this machine.
openspec.installDescription: The guided install runs the same npm global command shown below. Depending on your environment, npm may ask for administrator permissions.
openspec.successDescription: We detected a supported OpenSpec version on this machine. You can continue to download Hagicode Server without any extra confirmation.
openspec.manualFallback: Review the error, retry the guided install, or run the manual command above before verifying with openspec --version.
openspec.verifyDescription: When you enter this step, the app automatically checks whether OpenSpec is already available. If needed, you can still run the command below in your terminal to verify it manually.
openspec.successCriteria: Success criteria: openspec --version returns a version that is greater than 1.0 and less than 2.0.
openspec.permissionHint: If the guided install fails because of permissions or network access, use the manual command and retry when ready.
openspec.verifyHint: We automatically check for a supported version when this step opens. If no supported version is found, install OpenSpec and we will verify it again after installation.
openspec.verifyReady: Verification passed automatically. You can continue right away.
